updates.tweet_text:
One way traffic and free money today in #ES_F. As warned all day, just let that runner pay. Now +100 from yesterday's 7482 Reclaim Long. 8am targets given were 7547 (hit), 7565 (hit), 7577 (hit), 7597 final (en route).

Then, time for the major breakout to 7611, 7637+ https://t.co/VZqfZNDDwX

updates.tweet_text: @PX99128070 Its a reaction zone but I don't predict price paths. I just wait for elevator down sell, take the failed breakdown, hold runner. I know the range will breakout but as for when or how, that is up to price
updates.notes: Pure methodology/process commentary. No specific price levels named or actionable triggers identified.

updates.tweet_text: @Marcel22520116 It has to be a major shelf of support that breaks down to produce a large selloff. ITs very specific, has to be a multi-day very clear shelf with numerous tests that then collapses through. Can't just be any random support
updates.notes: Reply explaining a conceptual rule about shelf breakdowns; no specific price named or actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Today's final target was 7597. Targets given at 8am were 7547, 7565, 7577, then 7597. We hit ~7597, and rejected. Now, we will rebuild structure and then prep for breakout. We are building a 1.5 week flag which is 60% mature. Newsletter out soon with entries
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7547, 7565, 7577, 7597) are morning-plan levels or hindsight references to targets already given at 8am. No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @paigeclay0 Failed Breakdowns work best if the wick of the failed breakdown can also tag a big support. Then the failed breakdown is backed up by a direct support test
updates.notes: Reply tweet explaining FBD mechanics conceptually; no price named, no actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @bhargaval All elevator down sells are headline driven (they don't just happen randomly). I basically always hold a runner over the weekend though as long as my cost basis is low enough. All war dips will be bought without exception
updates.notes: Pure conversational reply about trade philosophy and holding runners over weekend. No actionable price levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @ewtnewbie You just buy it! Trading involves risk. As I said in the newsletter I don't take those trades (I only take Faild Breakdowns) but I give the levels and entries out and people can engage how they want. In this case we did flush it and recover though, so a little safer
